gaps: A snapshot field exposing normalized petition type or recoverability status would make sparse SCOTUS miscellaneous dockets easier to calibrate.
Case-specific issue metadata would help distinguish ordinary cert denials from petitions with institutional grant signals.
helpful: fedcourts paths resolved the authorized event and snapshot locations.
fedcourts stats provided a SCOTUS grant-rate calibration anchor.
fedcourts query confirmed the available corpus priors were not closely tailored to this sparse docket.
notes: The corpus tools were useful for base-rate calibration, but the sparse snapshot drove the uncertainty.
